items.q: What is the significance of Duchamp's 'Fountain'?
items.correct: It challenged the definition of art - the artist's choice, not their skill, becomes the artwork.
items.random_wrong: It is a study of plumbing.
items.why: Students miss the conceptual point. Duchamp didn't make the urinal - he selected and presented it. The radical claim is that ART IS DESIGNATION, opening the door to all conceptual art.

items.correct: Strong contrast between light and dark to model three-dimensional form.
items.why: Students think it's about subject matter (night scenes). Chiaroscuro is a TECHNIQUE using value contrast to suggest volume - exemplified by Caravaggio but used since the Renaissance to model form.

items.correct: Removing material from a solid block to reveal the form (e.g., carving stone).
items.why: Students confuse 'subtractive' with arithmetic. In sculpture, additive = building up (clay, welding); subtractive = taking away (carving). Michelangelo described 'freeing' figures from the marble - quintessential subtractive thinking.

items.q: Analysis using only form and ignoring biography is called:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Formalism. “Iconography” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: Stands for a larger moral idea
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Stands for a larger moral idea. “Has no story” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Year Delacroix painted Liberty Leading the People?
items.why: Exact answer: 1830. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
